channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: These satellites are on a mission to create the first fake solar eclipses. Here’s how
item.description: A pair of European satellites rocketed into orbit Thursday on the first mission to create artificial solar eclipses through fancy formation flying in space. Each fake eclipse should last six hours once operations begin next year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Berkeley changes zoning to encourage growth in innovation sector
item.description: Councilmembers voted Tuesday to permit labs studying biosafety levels 2 in mixed-use light industrial zoning areas while prohibiting BSL 3 and 4 uses in all parts of the city.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Lopez, The Mercury News

item.title: Cisco strengthens AWS integration to speed troubleshooting in multicloud environments
item.description: Cisco this week said it has bolstered integrations between its own hybrid cloud offerings and Amazon Web Services (AWS) to improve visibility and help enterprise customers more accurately troubleshoot application availability issues across multicloud networks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google DeepMind and World Labs unveil AI tools to create 3D spaces from simple prompts
item.description: Google DeepMind and startup World Labs this week both revealed previews of AI tools that can be used to create immersive 3D environments from simple prompts.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Burnout: A chronic epidemic in the IT industry
item.description: Burnout continues to be a widespread problem for IT organizations. It’s a complex problem that hasn’t improved since it peaked during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: This CEO sells low-cost drugs, and he says Big Pharma might not be the true adversary
item.description: For many Americans, pharmaceutical drugs are too expensive—and thanks to a system of complex and tedious bureaucracy, any effort for meaningful reform has been futile. Cost Plus Drugs wants to change that.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: For news, algorithmic social networks are a failed experiment
item.description: In 2016, when Twitter replaced its purely chronological timeline with one that used an algorithm to determine what users saw, I declared it to be a minor whoop at most—a 2.35 on a scale of 1 to 10.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: District of Columbia says Amazon secretly stopped fast deliveries to 2 predominantly Black ZIP codes
item.description: The District of Columbia sued Amazon on Wednesday, alleging the company secretly stopped providing its fastest delivery service to residents of two predominantly Black neighborhoods while still charging millions of dollars for a membership that promises the benefit.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump taps Gail Slater to lead DOJ antitrust division
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday tapped Gail Slater, a former adviser to Vice President-elect JD Vance, to lead the Justice Department’s antitrust division.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: If you had a Patreon account, you may qualify for this payment
item.description: Patreon will pay $7.25 million across users who had accounts and accessed videos on Patreon.com between April 1, 2016 and Sept. 23, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said he is “not that worried” about Musk's influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Kyndryl, Nokia expand AI, quantum-safe connectivity options
item.description: Kyndryl is extending its relationship with Nokia and expanding its menu of integration services by including more of Nokia’s data center networking products.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bad data costs Americans trillions. Let’s fix it with a renewed data strategy
item.description: Nick Hart of the Data Foundation and Suzette Kent, a former federal CIO, explain how using federal data could cut waste, create jobs and own America's future. The post Bad data costs Americans trillions. Let’s fix it with a renewed data strategy first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: The Future of Tech: AI-Driven Growth and Stabilizing Jobs Amidst Economic Volatility
item.description: IT staffing firm Motion Recruitment's 2025 Tech Salary Guide reveals stabilizing tech jobs, growing AI investments, and shifting hiring trends.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The FBI now says encryption is good for you
item.description: Apple has faced an unequal battle in recent years as some lawmakers, the FBI, and regulators insist that the company create backdoors through which to access messages and other parts of its platform.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to bulk delete spam and old text messages on iPhone
item.description: Tech wizard Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to delete old unwanted texts and spam messages from cluttered inboxes of an iPhone in bulk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hands on with Microsoft’s Windows Recall: Not impressive yet
item.description: When Microsoft announced Recall earlier this year, the move took the tech world by storm — and not in a good way. Recall is supposed to help you find almost anything you see on your PC by taking screenshots in the background and turning your everyday Windows usage into a searchable database.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Founder of failed crypto lending platform Celsius Network pleads guilty to fraud charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The founder and former CEO of the failed cryptocurrency lending platform Celsius Network could face decades in prison after pleading guilty Tuesday to federal fraud charges, admitting that he misled customers about the business.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Agencies still struggling to meet digital accessibility standards
item.description: GSA has said that limited staffing and resources are key drivers of agencies’ ongoing challenges with digital accessibility and Section 508 conformance. The post Agencies still struggling to meet digital accessibility standards first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Arista adds intelligent switch stacking, management for campus networks
item.description: Arista Networks is adding new features to its campus networking portfolio to help enterprise customers more easily group and manage switches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: TikTok defends handling of Romania election content in grilling by EU lawmakers
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — TikTok took down several networks that tried to meddle in Romania's elections, executives said Tuesday as they defended the company's election integrity measures to European Union lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN and STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Dark Side of Open-Washing in AI: A Threat to Transparency and Collaboration
item.description: The dangers of open-washing in AI, a practice that prioritizes profit over transparency and collaboration, affecting innovation and competition in the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Linux fundamentals: Viewing files, commands, processes and systems
item.description: Linux provides a useful array of commands for managing file permissions, getting information on commands, displaying running processes and displaying details on the system itself.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Pentagon has a new way to tackle disconnect between financial and acquisition systems
item.description: It's not a silver bullet for DoD's challenges with business IT systems, but the department thinks it's overcome one big barrier involved in sharing budget data. The post Pentagon has a new way to tackle disconnect between financial and acquisition systems first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: AWS PartyRock updated with free daily usage for developers
item.description: Amazon Web Services has updated AWS PartyRock, its mostly free low code tool for building generative AI applications with a new app search function and the ability to build document processing into applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Refactoring AI code: The good, the bad, and the weird
item.description: Generative AI is no longer a novelty in the software development world: it’s being increasingly used as an assistant (and sometimes a free agent) to write code running in real-world production. But every developer knows that writing new code from scratch is only a small part of their daily work.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Artificial intelligence changes across the US
item.description: As more companies implement artificial intelligence for daily tasks, some industry leaders are working to push back against certain aspects of the technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, Fox 5
